approach: Leakage-safe exact 12-vertex boundary repair on eight authenticated order-42 controls with canonical novelty as the fail-closed gate.

strategy.mechanism: Freeze a fingerprint-selected induced order-30 graph, solve all 426 boundary edges exactly subject to Ramsey clauses and minimum Hamming distance 60, then dual-check and canonicalize the first model.

hypothesis: At least one of eight predeclared 12-vertex boundaries admits a valid Ramsey(5,5,42) first SAT model at labelled distance at least 60 outside all supplied 656 canonical classes.
discriminating_test: Accept discovery only if a decoded graph passes independent Python and C forbidden-five-set scans, preserves the fixed boundary scope, and its nauty canonical label is absent from all 656 controls.
summary: All eight formulas were SAT and yielded valid graphs at boundary distances 178--232, but all eight canonicalized to distinct supplied classes. A separate NetworkX/DIMACS cold audit confirmed all 4,061,806 physical clauses, graph and complement clique number four, every fixed induced order-30 graph, and exact isomorphism to the identified control. No new graph or Ramsey bound was obtained.
rationale: Canonical membership was the predeclared discovery gate, so eight rediscoveries falsify the one-model-per-boundary hypothesis at exactly its declared scope. The models nevertheless provide exact witnesses for eight transitions between distinct supplied classes through common induced order-30 graphs.
claims: The first exact SAT model in each of eight declared 12-vertex boundaries is a valid Ramsey(5,5,42) graph and a supplied-corpus rediscovery.
The eight source/target class pairs listed in the cold report share an induced order-30 graph, witnessed by retained boundary assignments at labelled distances 178--232.

The public selected-layer r45extreme archive cannot by itself supply universal interior-layer I3 feature bounds, and the 1997 displayed I2/I3 LP is applied there to R(4,6), not published as an order-45 R(5,5) baseline.
evidence: artifacts/novel42_boundary_repair/* contains eight graph6 witnesses, canonical labels, compressed physical CNFs, and compressed total models.
docs/novel42-boundary-repair.md contains the formulation, transition table, limitations, hashes, and replay commands.
sources/rowpair/subgraph-counting-r55.pdf and https://users.cecs.anu.edu.au/~bdm/data/ramsey.html support the I2/I3 route rejection.
next_steps: Implement canonical counterexample-guided boundary-model blocking on retained trial 1.
Predeclare a 64-model budget; dual-check and canonicalize every model, and block each supplied-known 426-edge assignment before continuing.
Do not repeat the same unblocked one-model portfolio with arbitrary additional seeds.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260721-141228-a33851: failed closed before SAT because temporary graph6 records lacked separators; no claim.
.proof-experiments/20260721-141324-22844d: accepted production, eight SAT-valid supplied-class rediscoveries in 146.234 seconds total.
.proof-experiments/20260721-141801-cf3c9d: independent cold audit passed in 16.089 seconds.
.proof-experiments/20260721-142133-c076d7: compressed replay passed in 12.833 seconds and emitted a byte-identical audit report.
independent_checker: checkers/novel42_boundary_audit.py imports none of the producer and uses NetworkX graph6 parsing, maximal-clique enumeration in graph and complement, NetworkX isomorphism to the manifest target, and direct evaluation of every DIMACS clause under each total model. The producer also required byte-identical outputs from checkers/checker_a.py and independently implemented checkers/checker_b.c.
transfer_insights: Exact destroy-and-repair -> nonlocal rewiring should escape a known order-42 basin -> eight first models instead traversed to eight other supplied classes, showing large labelled distance is not a novelty proxy.

Counterexample-guided synthesis -> retain each known-class rediscovery as a concrete counterexample -> next test should block complete labelled boundary assignments within one fixed CNF rather than add seeds.

established_facts.claim: Eight predeclared exact boundary first models are valid supplied-corpus rediscoveries.
established_facts.evidence: Production report plus byte-identical cold-audit replay.
established_facts.scope: Exactly source records 21,36,41,83,126,128,192,216 and their fingerprint-selected 12-vertex boundaries.

established_facts.claim: Eight distinct source/target supplied-class pairs share an induced order-30 graph.
established_facts.evidence: The frozen-boundary checks and independent target isomorphisms in artifacts/novel42_boundary_repair_cold_audit.json.
established_facts.scope: Exactly the eight retained transitions, not the full corpus.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Use the first unblocked SAT model from the same eight boundaries as a supplied-corpus-novel discovery mechanism.
ruled_out.scope: The exact formulas, solver seeds, and first models retained in this packet.
ruled_out.reason: Every first model is a valid supplied class.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Add canonical model blocking or a materially different novelty-enforcing mechanism.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat r45extreme selected-layer feature extrema as universal order-45 I3 bounds.
ruled_out.reason: The archive omits interior edge layers required for universal fixed-order/fixed-edge extrema.
ruled_out.evidence: Authoritative ANU data index and the primary I2/I3 paper.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Supply complete catalogues or proved global feature bounds for the missing layers.

open_leads.description: Canonical counterexample-guided exact repair within one retained boundary.
open_leads.rationale: Each rediscovery supplies an exact boundary assignment to block, directly addressing the observed failure mode.
open_leads.next_experiment: Enumerate at most 64 trial-1 models, block each supplied-known 426-edge assignment, and require dual checking plus canonical novelty for success.

blocker: Unblocked exact solving preferentially returns supplied classes, and the packet has no isomorphism-class blocking encoding or exhaustive boundary certificate.
reopen_condition: Install checked boundary-model blocking with a predeclared model budget; a global boundary exclusion additionally requires a completed checked UNSAT proof.
reopen_evidence: The exact boundary CNFs, authenticated controls, dual full graph checkers, canonical membership gate, and cold model checker now exist.

continuation.objective: Test whether canonical counterexample-guided blocking escapes the supplied classes within retained trial 1.
continuation.first_action: Extend the producer or add a replayable enumerator that appends a 426-literal block for each verified known boundary assignment and retains every model/canonical label up to a budget of 64.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop on a dual-checked supplied-corpus-novel graph, 64 verified rediscoveries, any checker discrepancy, or an uncertified solver conclusion.

strategy_proposals.mechanism: Iteratively solve one frozen boundary, canonicalize each valid model, and block its complete labelled 426-edge assignment when it is supplied-known.
strategy_proposals.hypothesis: After finitely many supplied-class assignments are blocked, the same exact boundary yields a valid graph outside all 656 controls.
strategy_proposals.discriminating_test: A predeclared 64-model run produces a dual-checked canonical-novel graph or an exact known-class rediscovery ledger.
strategy_proposals.rationale: It responds to the measured failure mode and changes the solver's admissible model set rather than merely its random seed.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: Can an exact nonlocal boundary repair of known Ramsey(5,5,42) graphs reach a supplied-corpus-novel order-42 basin?
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: The packet gives the first internally audited exact boundary-repair benchmark in this campaign and eight explicit cross-class order-30 overlap witnesses; it does not claim historical novelty.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: A third party replays the compressed packet, obtains the same cold-audit hash, and confirms the exact transition table and scope.

closest_prior_work.difference: The authoritative page supplies the 656 controls; this packet records exact boundary transitions among eight of their classes, not a new census member.
The historical work constructs and studies order-42 graphs; this bounded benchmark tests one exact nonlocal repair mechanism with explicit fail-closed novelty.

novelty_searches.finding: The page says the 656 are supplied examples and more order-42 graphs could exist; it does not advertise this boundary-transition dataset.

novelty_searches.source: Maintained Dynamic Survey and 2026 primary upper-bound paper
novelty_searches.finding: The maintained bound remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46; this packet changes neither endpoint.

external_channel.acceptance_path: Seek specialist interest only after canonical CEGAR produces a new graph or a theorem-strength bounded exclusion.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol acted as principal investigator. Supplied GPT-5.6 Terra literature-strategy and experiment-verification memos were advisory and promoted with provenance; no new subagent was spawned. Deterministic tools were Python 3.12.3, GCC 13.3.0, CaDiCaL 1.7.3, nauty labelg, NetworkX, gzip, SHA-256, jq, pdftotext, web search of primary/authoritative sources, and the computational-researcher experiment harness. No CAS, numerical optimizer, proof assistant, external publication, or system-level change was used.
